reasoning: Q3 has a critical correctness bug (zone map block index mismatch) causing 0 rows instead of 10. Once fixed, there's a 4-5x performance gap vs DuckDB (386ms vs 78ms). With 2 iterations remaining, we must fix correctness AND aggressively tackle multiple bottlenecks: single-threaded scans (wasting 63/64 cores), inefficient hash table design, and suboptimal filtering.
focus_areas: Fix zone map block index calculation (CRITICAL correctness fix)
Parallelize customer and orders scans (8-16x speedup expected)
Optimize hash table design: build unified table, avoid vector allocations
Zone map optimization: early exit after first non-skipped lineitem block
Pre-size aggregation hash tables to avoid rehashing during merge
strategy_notes: CORRECTNESS FIRST: Fix the zone map block_idx calculation at line 338 - use actual row positions (i / 100000) instead of block_start-based calculation. This is non-negotiable. THEN performance: The query is single-threaded in critical phases (customer scan, orders scan) despite 64 cores available. Parallelizing these alone should yield 8-16x speedup. The hash table design has structural issues (vector allocations per order, two separate tables) that cause memory overhead and poor cache locality. Fix all selected issues simultaneously - the Query Optimizer can handle multiple optimizations. Skip the high-risk SIMD vectorization (rec #6) for now since the lower-hanging fruit (parallelism, hash table design) should close most of the gap. Focus on correctness + parallelism + data structure efficiency.
notes: Be aggressive with parallelism - this is the biggest opportunity. After fixing correctness, the ~4x performance gap can be closed by parallelizing scans (currently single-threaded) and optimizing hash table design. DuckDB's 78ms target is achievable.
